match.userMessage: Plan a trip from LAX to JFK

toolCalls.name: task
toolCalls.arguments: {"role":"research","task_description":"Gather destination intel on LAX and JFK airports for a coast-to-coast trip."}

match.userMessage: Gather destination intel on LAX and JFK airports for a coast-to-coast trip.

response.content: LAX sprawls across nine terminals while JFK runs six under the AirTrain loop. New York in spring is mild, mid-50s to low-70s with occasional showers, so pack layers for the JFK arrival.

match.userMessage: Plan a trip from LAX to JFK

toolCalls.name: task
toolCalls.arguments: {"role":"booking","task_description":"Find nonstop flight options from LAX to JFK and back for one adult."}

match.userMessage: Find nonstop flight options from LAX to JFK and back for one adult.

response.content: Three nonstop options: a low-cost JetBlue red-eye (~$210), a balanced mid-morning Delta departure (~$330), and a premium United widebody with extra legroom (~$780). All run roughly 5h30 westbound to eastbound.

match.userMessage: Plan a trip from LAX to JFK

toolCalls.name: task
toolCalls.arguments: {"role":"itinerary","task_description":"Synthesize a final LAX to JFK trip plan from the research and booking notes."}

match.userMessage: Synthesize a final LAX to JFK trip plan from the research and booking notes.

response.content: Take the balanced mid-morning Delta nonstop from LAX to JFK; arrive about two hours early and ride the AirTrain into the city. Expect mild spring weather in New York, so bring a light jacket and a compact umbrella.

match.userMessage: Plan a trip from LAX to JFK

response.content: All set — I researched both airports, found flights, and drafted an itinerary. Recommended plan: book the balanced mid-morning nonstop from LAX to JFK, arrive about two hours early, and take the AirTrain into the city. Pack for mild spring weather in New York.
